derivation: OS is the resource manager: allocates CPU, memory, I/O and files; processing data / printing / error display are application-level tasks.
'more' is the classic Unix pager showing one screenful at a time; cat dumps whole file, ls/dir list directories.
passwd is the Unix password-change command; other three are not real commands.
ls lists directory contents in Unix; dir and dir /w are DOS, lsd is not standard.
vi is the POSIX-standard Unix editor; emacs is common but not the standard, edit is DOS, cat is not an editor.

Canonical vi modes are command mode and insert mode; the only option pairing insert with a non-typing mode is 'Edit and Insert' (edit=command mode), but the exact term 'command' is absent so 'None of the above' is arguable. Ambiguous item.
A cylinder is the set of same-numbered tracks on all platter surfaces, i.e. vertically aligned tracks reachable without head movement.
Each of A, B, C lists only genuine storage devices (disk, tape, CD, hard disk, optical, floppy), so 'All the above' is correct.
DNS forward lookup resolves hostname -> IP; option A states the reverse (PTR lookup), which is not the main function.
Modem = modulator/demodulator: digital<->analog signal conversion over the line.
